executive_summary: Global systemic risk remains moderate with physical energy reserves maintaining high operational stability despite speculative noise in the technology sector. The discrepancy between information panic and material reality in the Taiwan Strait suggests localised narrative volatility rather than actual physical disruption. Consequently current supply chain and economic fracture vectors do not present an immediate threat to global network integrity.
correlations: The minor fluctuation in the Middle East energy spike has failed to register on global fuel reserves indicating high systemic buffer capacity.
